name: deploying-to-production
description: Automate creating a GitHub repository and deploying a web project to Vercel. Use when the user asks to deploy a website/app to production, publish a project, or set up GitHub + Vercel deployment.
capabilities: Run npm build and verify the build completes without errors
Create a new GitHub repository for the project
Initialize git, add remote, and push code to GitHub
Deploy the GitHub repository to Vercel automatically
Verify the live deployment is accessible and working
Handle deployment failures by diagnosing and retrying
author: ZhanlinCui
securitySummary: Both static findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' detection refers to a standard 'npm run build' command which is a legitimate build step in web deployment workflows. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flag was triggered by YAML frontmatter text that contains no cryptographic code. This skill provides safe, read-only deployment guidance.
valueStatement: This skill automates the entire deployment process by guiding users through creating a GitHub repository and deploying to Vercel, eliminating manual deployment steps and reducing errors.
limitations: Only supports Vercel as the deployment target
Does not configure custom domains or SSL certificates
Does not set up environment variables or secrets
Requires user to have Vercel and GitHub accounts already set up

useCases.targetUser: Developers who want to quickly share their web projects online
useCases.title: Quick Project Deployment
useCases.description: Deploy a new web project to production in minutes without manually configuring git or deployment settings.

useCases.title: CI/CD Workflow Setup
useCases.description: Set up automated deployment pipelines for continuous integration and delivery.

useCases.title: Client Project Handover
useCases.description: Deploy client projects to production with proper version control and live URLs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Deployment Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy this website to production using the deploying-to-production skill.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to deploy their web project to Vercel

promptTemplates.title: New Project Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a new Next.js/React project in the current directory. Please deploy it to production using GitHub and Vercel.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has a new web project needing deployment

promptTemplates.title: Existing Project Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: This project already has a GitHub repository. Please deploy it to Vercel and verify the deployment is working.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to connect an existing repo to Vercel

promptTemplates.title: Full Setup with Verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up GitHub and Vercel deployment for this project. Create the repository, push the code, deploy to Vercel, and verify the live site is working.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants complete deployment with verification

bestPractices: Always run the build locally first to catch any compilation errors before deployment
Verify the deployment URL after completion to ensure the site is actually working
Check the Vercel dashboard for deployment logs if issues occur
antiPatterns: Skipping the build verification step can lead to deploying broken code
Not verifying the deployment may miss runtime errors that only appear in production
Attempting to deploy without proper git configuration can cause version control issues

faq.question: Does this skill work with all web frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill works with any web project that uses npm run build, including Next.js, React, Vue, Svelte, and other modern frameworks.

faq.question: Do I need a Vercel account?
faq.answer: Yes, you must have a Vercel account linked to your GitHub account before using this skill.

faq.question: Can I deploy to a custom domain?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on initial deployment. Custom domain configuration must be done manually in the Vercel dashboard.

faq.question: What happens if the build fails?
faq.answer: The skill will stop and report the build errors. You need to fix the errors locally before attempting deployment again.

faq.question: Can I redeploy after the initial deployment?
faq.answer: Yes, you can run this skill again to redeploy. It will push updated code to the existing GitHub repository.

faq.question: Is this skill secure?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill only runs npm build and interacts with GitHub and Vercel APIs. It does not execute arbitrary code or access sensitive systems.
